jokes.text: Would you describe these pieces more savory or succulent... It's definitely way too over cooked to be succulent, but it's still pretty delicious... Now, what if we insert it in beer? Do you think that would help like regain it's succulence.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing meat with pretentious culinary terminology
jokes.punchline: They're talking about stolen meat like sommelier wine experts
jokes.explanation: Sets up the characters' pretentious attitude toward stolen meat, establishing the tone of their oblivious self-importance

jokes.text: Bite my bird.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what they're eating
jokes.punchline: Dee's bird-related insult as response to interruption
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee bird reference, economical character-specific insult

jokes.text: Someone who sweats as much as you should not work with food.
jokes.setup: Charlie offering food to share
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disgusted observation about Charlie's hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis condescension and Charlie grossness dynamic

jokes.text: Spit it out! Spit it! Spit it out! Now! You too. Spit! Come on. Right there.
jokes.setup: Frank discovering people eating his meat
jokes.punchline: Frank demanding they spit out the meat like children
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of treating adults like misbehaving children

jokes.text: I killed the dear, I should get to eat it. That's the natural order.
jokes.setup: Arguing over who gets to eat the meat
jokes.punchline: Frank claiming primitive hunting rights over store-bought deer
jokes.explanation: Frank's delusional tough-guy persona claiming primal hunting rights

jokes.text: 10 point buck. Ping! Right between the sorrowful little eyes.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming to be a great hunter
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description with 'sorrowful little eyes'
jokes.explanation: Frank's mock-heroic hunting tale with unexpectedly poetic language

jokes.text: You know, a really great hunter would go after something that can hunt him back... Like a man!
jokes.setup: Discussion about what makes a good hunter
jokes.punchline: Escalation to hunting humans as the logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that drives the episode's central premise, very Mac and Dennis logic

jokes.text: Like a man! Hell yeah, dude, a man? Don't even joke about hunting a man
jokes.setup: The suggestion of hunting something dangerous
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to hunting humans and Frank's alarm
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and structure, sets up the entire episode premise

jokes.text: Yeah? I was hunted once. I just came back from Nam. I was hitching through Oregon and some cop started harassing me. Next thing you know, I had a whole army of cops chasing me through the woods. I had to take 'em all out. It was a bloodbath.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming personal hunting experience
jokes.punchline: He's literally describing the plot of Rambo
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Frank's delusion/lies revealed through movie plot recycling

jokes.text: That's Rambo, dude... You just described the plot of Rambo.
jokes.setup: Frank's elaborate war story
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation by identifying the movie source
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncture of Frank's delusion with matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: That's not the first time you've described your life in the way of John Rambo's like.
jokes.setup: Frank caught telling Rambo story
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is a pattern of Frank's behavior
jokes.explanation: Deepens the character joke by making it a recurring trait

jokes.text: I can't believe that dick horrible put the lock on the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Complaining about Frank locking the fridge
jokes.punchline: Mangled insult 'dick horrible' instead of proper profanity
jokes.explanation: Dee's characteristic verbal fumbling creates accidental humor

jokes.text: What I did, I slopped through the radiator for minute... not warmed it.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he prepared the meat
jokes.punchline: Charlie's gross, nonsensical cooking method using a radiator
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie weirdness - using radiator as cooking appliance

jokes.text: By the way, you know what you've just eaten, right?... That, which you have just eaten, that, which your teeth have just torn apart. Your taste buds have savored, that was human meat.
jokes.setup: Frank's dramatic buildup about what they ate
jokes.punchline: Overly theatrical revelation of 'human meat'
jokes.explanation: Frank's melodramatic delivery makes the reveal both horrifying and absurd

jokes.text: Yeah, ok... You're so stupid!... Well, thank you for the human meat, Frank. Thank you for our human meat... It was delicious.
jokes.setup: Frank's dramatic human meat revelation
jokes.punchline: Complete dismissal and sarcastic thanks
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Frank's dramatic moment

jokes.text: I got a guy... You got a human meat guy?... I got a guide for everything, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming to have acquired human meat
jokes.punchline: Frank matter-of-factly having 'a guy for everything' including human meat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Frank's criminal connections to absurd extremes

jokes.text: That wasn't human meat though, right? No~~~!
jokes.setup: After Frank leaves, uncertainty about the meat
jokes.punchline: Dee's horrified realization they might have actually eaten human
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective horror realization beat

jokes.text: Check out his legs. See how they're almost tierd? They're like pretty much back to perfect... And check out his cane. Looks like he's whittled it into some sort of defensive tool.
jokes.setup: Dennis evaluating Cricket as hunting prey
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of Cricket's fitness as a human hunting target
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy of evaluating a person like hunting game, perfectly Dennis

jokes.text: You're gonna harvest my organs, aren't you?... You're gonna rope me and you're gonna harvest my organs.
jokes.setup: Cricket sensing something wrong
jokes.punchline: Cricket's paranoid but not wrong assumption about their intentions
jokes.explanation: Cricket's justified paranoia about their treatment of him

jokes.text: Yeah, we are. Stack on 'em... No, we're gonna hunt you.
jokes.setup: Cricket thinking they'll harvest organs
jokes.punchline: Revealing hunting is somehow the better alternative
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny misdirection - hunting is presented as the better option

jokes.text: Why?... Uh.. I'll spend a lot less time asking questions, more time running.
jokes.setup: Cricket asking why they're hunting him
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact advice to focus on survival over questions
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of horrific hunting advice

jokes.text: Well, I suppose we do some tea-bag related.
jokes.setup: Mac asking what happens when they catch Cricket
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually planning sexual assault as hunting trophy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their casual cruelty toward Cricket

jokes.text: I couldn't sleep last night, could you?... And I'll tell you why... Yesterday.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee meeting to discuss something serious
jokes.punchline: Building dramatic tension over their meat consumption
jokes.explanation: Sets up their shared obsession over the meat question

jokes.text: The taste! The taste, Charlie, I tried everything... I can't get the taste out of my mouth!
jokes.setup: Discussing their reaction to the meat
jokes.punchline: Dee's desperate obsession with the lingering taste
jokes.explanation: Building the cannibalism paranoia with Dee's desperation

jokes.text: Hey. We got monkey. Oh. Well, we'll look at that. Great! Uh, one monkey also then, please.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggesting monkey meat to compare flavors
jokes.punchline: Exotic meat shop actually having monkey available
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of their meat investigation to exotic options

jokes.text: I'm so exited, feel my nips... They're, like, super hard, right? You can cut glass with these bad boys.
jokes.setup: Mac expressing excitement about hunting
jokes.punchline: Mac's weird fixation on his excited nipples
jokes.explanation: Peak Mac weirdness - his bizarre physical expressions of excitement

jokes.text: You know you hunt a man, he can snap like a toig. Next thing you know, he's up at night, he's burning down a village of Nam, he's killing everything that moves, everything that lives!
jokes.setup: Frank warning about hunting humans
jokes.punchline: Frank again describing Rambo plot as personal experience
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Frank's Rambo confusion, building the running gag

jokes.text: You're talking about Rambo again... You're thinking about John Rambo's life, every time.
jokes.setup: Frank's another Rambo story
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis matter-of-factly calling out the pattern
jokes.explanation: Strengthens the running gag by showing others recognize Frank's pattern

jokes.text: That's how you become a cannibal, Dee. Like, you get one taste of delicious, delicious human meat, none of these stuff ever satisfies you, ever again for the rest of your life.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee trying different meats unsuccessfully
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confident explanation of cannibalism addiction
jokes.explanation: Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery of insane cannibalism theory

jokes.text: Well, then I guess Jaws IV is stupid, ok? 'cause that's exact same plot.
jokes.setup: Dee dismissing his cannibalism theory
jokes.punchline: Charlie comparing cannibalism addiction to Jaws IV plot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison mixing movie logic with cannibalism

jokes.text: I think we need to try a piece of human flesh... The morgue? Hear me out... I got a hot plate.
jokes.setup: Needing to test if they ate human meat
jokes.punchline: Casually planning morgue break-in with cooking equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly matter-of-fact planning of extremely disturbing activity

jokes.text: It's just like our normal lives, except at the end of it we get to put our nuts in some dude's mouth.
jokes.setup: Mac describing hunting as awesome
jokes.punchline: Mac reducing hunting to sexual assault opportunity
jokes.explanation: Mac's casual cruelty and weird sexual fixations

jokes.text: What's a gorilla mask? You put some sticky over his face, and then sprinkle it with shaved pubes.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about Mac's gorilla mask idea
jokes.punchline: Mac's detailed explanation of disturbing sexual prank
jokes.explanation: Mac's enthusiasm for increasingly disturbing pranks

jokes.text: I laser. It's like a turtle shell down there.
jokes.setup: Mac asking where to get pubes for the prank
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing his extreme grooming habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity moment with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Ok, how did his legs just do that? I'm not doing that, Dennis. No, that was incredibly dangerous.
jokes.setup: Cricket apparently escaping with surprising agility
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis shocked by Cricket's unexpected athleticism
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing Cricket's unexpected capabilities

jokes.text: The hot plate is.. because.. our friend was a.. a chef... of small.. many small items on hot plate... Right, right, right. Look, I respect the act. I'll give you guys the same deal like I gave every else. 15 bucks get you 10 minutes alone with the bodies.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee trying to explain the hot plate to morgue worker
jokes.punchline: Morgue worker assuming they're necrophiliacs and offering standard rates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - their innocent explanation sounds worse than the truth

jokes.text: I must say thought, I've never seen a guy and a girl bang one of these taste at the same time.
jokes.setup: Morgue worker assuming they're necrophiliacs
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of this being unusual necrophilia scenario
jokes.explanation: Deepens the misunderstanding with casual necrophilia commentary

jokes.text: I like yogurt up my ass and a popsicle stick in my mouth, that's all.
jokes.setup: Defending himself against being called weird
jokes.punchline: Morgue worker's casual revelation of his own bizarre habits
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation with morgue worker's own weird sexual preferences

jokes.text: It's not because he's black, though, right?... No! What, no! I still think so, no!... It's because he's dead, right?... Good, good, good. Now, I got a question for you. We're racist if we don't eat this guy?
jokes.setup: Looking at dead bodies and hesitating
jokes.punchline: Escalating into worrying about racist implications of their cannibalism preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of their moral confusion into absurd territory

jokes.text: I generally, I don't eat dark meat. No, I've preferred the white meat. I always have... The problem is I'm gonna have a really hard time for both cannibals and racist.
jokes.setup: Discussing their food preferences with dead bodies
jokes.punchline: Accidental double meaning of meat preferences creating racist implications
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that creates escalating moral panic

jokes.text: Cannibalism? Racism, Dee? That's not for us. You know, those are the decisions that're best left to the suits on Washington.
jokes.setup: Dee worrying about being racist cannibals
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating moral decisions like political policy issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - deferring moral responsibility to government

jokes.text: They drew first blood, not you. They drew first blood!
jokes.setup: Frank trying to help Cricket against Mac and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Frank again quoting Rambo, this time to Cricket
jokes.explanation: Continues Frank's Rambo pattern, now applied to Cricket's situation

jokes.text: Tag! You're it!... Tag, you're it? And a knife? What the hell is this, man?
jokes.setup: Cricket turning the tables on his hunters
jokes.punchline: Cricket treating serious violence like a children's game
jokes.explanation: Dark humor of mixing childhood games with violence

jokes.text: You gorilla masked me?... Yes! Bro, you gotta admit. I had so much more awesome than tea-bagging.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizing Mac performed the gorilla mask on him
jokes.punchline: Mac's pride in executing his disturbing prank idea
jokes.explanation: Payoff of Mac's earlier disturbing prank planning

jokes.text: You put your balls in my mouth while I was sleeping?... Yeah, man, twice... That's rape... Yeah, I know... That is borderline rape.
jokes.setup: Dennis discovering Mac tea-bagged him while unconscious
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual admission and Dennis's understatement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their casual sexual assault and calling it 'borderline'

jokes.text: Dee, would you calm down? You're eating that cheese steak like a some kind of giant bird, you know?
jokes.setup: Dee eating aggressively due to her 'hunger'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual bird comparison for Dee
jokes.explanation: Standard Dee-bird joke but delivered naturally in context

jokes.text: We gotta find a guy. We gotta kill that guy, and we gotta eat it. Somebody, little rotten maybe... Someone no in the world could ever care about.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee needing fresh meat instead of morgue bodies
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact planning of murder for food
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual escalation to murder with practical considerations

jokes.text: [Homeless person appears begging] Small change? Hungry Please help.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee discussing needing someone nobody would miss
jokes.punchline: Perfect victim immediately appears on cue
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing - their target appears immediately

jokes.text: I can't do this. They're not coming off, seriously... I used airplane glue, bro. That's gonna be your look for a while.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to remove the gorilla mask
jokes.punchline: Mac used permanent glue for his prank
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Mac's prank to permanent consequences

jokes.text: If we don't eat this kid, we're gonna die. You know it... but it's wrong to do it... Damn it! Damn it! So, you're gonna eat that kid.
jokes.setup: Considering eating the homeless person
jokes.punchline: Their moral struggle over cannibalism with practical concerns
jokes.explanation: Peak moral confusion and cannibalism desperation

jokes.text: You ate raccoon meat. I told you it was people, because I wanted to freak you out, because you're stealing my food.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee's entire cannibalism panic
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing it was just raccoon meat all along
jokes.explanation: Major reveal that deflates the entire cannibalism subplot

jokes.text: You probably got a tapeworm. Raccoon meat is lousy with parasites.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about continued hunger and stomach pains
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact explanation of parasite infection
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual delivery of disgusting medical information

jokes.text: I just don't understand your generation... you guys are always touching each other's nipples, putting your balls in each other's mouths.
jokes.setup: Frank confused by Mac and Dennis's behavior
jokes.punchline: Frank's generational commentary on their homoerotic activities
jokes.explanation: Frank's confused generational observation about their weird behavior

jokes.text: I gotta chop off that pieces of that fellow calf muscle of yours, Frank. And I'm gonna eat it.
jokes.setup: Charlie still believing Frank has human meat
jokes.punchline: Charlie threatening to eat Frank to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Charlie's continued cannibalism obsession targeting Frank directly

jokes.text: Guess it's just us. It's just you and us, and a.. couple pairs of sour sweaty balls.
jokes.setup: Left alone with Cricket after the chaos
jokes.punchline: Their casual return to sexual assault plans
jokes.explanation: Dark ending that shows their return to terrible behavior, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: It's just you and us, and a.. couple pairs of sour sweaty balls.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis alone with Cricket after the chaos
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual return to sexual assault plans
jokes.explanation: Returns to the episode's sexual assault theme as the conclusion
